description_long: This is a brightly colored biplane with a striking blue and yellow paint scheme, featuring large circular red and yellow insignias on the upper and lower wings as well as the tail fin. The aircraft has two stacked wings connected by struts, a single propeller at the nose, and a fixed undercarriage with two main wheels and a small tail skid. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers towards the tail, which has a conventional horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, likely designed for a single pilot. The propeller and nose cone are painted in matching blue and yellow, complementing the overall color scheme. The surface appears smooth and glossy, suggesting a metal or composite construction with painted finishes.
description: A vivid blue and yellow biplane with red insignias, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear. Features two stacked wings and a single front propeller.
description_view_0: The left side view highlights the stacked biplane wings, propeller, and tail with visible insignias on the wings and tail fin.
description_view_1: The front view shows the dual wings, open cockpit, and the full span of the landing gear, with the propeller and nose cone clearly visible.
description_view_2: The right side view mirrors the left, displaying the wing structure, tail, and insignias, with the propeller angled slightly.
description_view_3: The rear view emphasizes the tail assembly, landing gear, and the open cockpit, with the wings and propeller seen from behind.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
